headline: Sayin Delivers in Clutch as Ohio State Edges Washington
body: Ohio State's Julian Sayin proved why he earned player of the game honors, threading three touchdown passes through a Washington secondary that struggled to maintain coverage throughout the afternoon. The Buckeyes' quarterback completed 24 of 37 attempts for 266 yards, managing the game with precision when it mattered most. Washington's Williams Jr. kept pace statistically—260 yards on 23 completions with two scores of his own—but the margin came down to execution in the red zone and one costly turnover that never materialized into points.

The rushing game told Ohio State's story of control. Judkins churned out 67 yards on 11 carries, establishing enough ground presence to keep Washington's pass rush honest. By contrast, Washington managed only 56 rushing yards, a statistic that forced Williams Jr. to beat Ohio State through the air. When a team can't establish rhythm on the ground, the passing lanes tighten, and Ohio State's defense made that principle pay. Smith's six-catch, 84-yard performance included a touchdown that gave the Buckeyes breathing room late.

The Huskies' fumble on the morning belonged in the turnover margin column, yet it never cost them points. What did cost them was a three-point deficit that Ohio State's defense, led by Roker's four tackles, refused to surrender. This was a game where field position and efficiency separated two teams with similar total yardage. Ohio State's staff engineered better situational football when the field compressed, and that discipline held through the final whistle.
turningPoint: Sayin's third touchdown pass to Smith with under five minutes remaining extended Ohio State's lead to 23-14, giving Washington no margin for error on its final drive.

unsungHero.name: Judkins
unsungHero.why: His 11-carry workload established the ground game that kept Washington's pass rush from collapsing the pocket, yet Sayin's three touchdown passes captured all the spotlight in a three-point game decided by execution.
